articles.title: heatwave in Uttar Pradesh pushes temperatures up to 46°C, Moradabad residents face severe challenges - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
Uttar Pradesh is experiencing severe heatwave conditions, with temperatures ranging from 43°C to 46°C across multiple districts, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported on Sunday, May 24, 2026. The heatwave, including severe heat conditions, is expected to persist particularly in eastern parts of the state.
Residents of Moradabad described worsening conditions as intense heat combined with rising humidity has made daily life difficult. Many are venturing outdoors only for essential activities, relying on cold beverages and water to find relief.
Akhil Khan, who went out to purchase medicine for his child, stated, “It is extremely hot today. Stepping out on the road is becoming very difficult. It was necessary to come out, otherwise I would have stayed at home.”
Khilendra, another local resident, added, “It’s very hot, and the humidity is extremely high. We mostly stay indoors and only come out for urgent necessities. Otherwise, we finish our work in the morning or evening.”
Advice to take precautions was echoed by Ashok Mittal, who said, “The temperature has risen significantly today, causing a lot of difficulty. In this heat, one should drink plenty of water, take care and avoid stepping out in the sun unless it is essential.”
At several roadside locations in Moradabad, people were observed stopping to receive sharbat distributed outside a Gurdwara to provide relief from the heat. Gurjit Singh explained, “Due to the extreme heat, the Gurdwara is distributing sharbat to provide some relief. The temperature is at its peak this season, which is why we are serving it.”
A passerby praised the initiative, saying, “The heat is intense and the sun is scorching. This arrangement for distributing sherbet is a very good initiative. People are stopping to drink it to get some relief.”
Meanwhile, Congress leader Supriya Shrinate criticised the Uttar Pradesh government over power cuts during the heatwave, highlighting widespread distress among residents. She stated, “It’s unbearably hot, but in Uttar Pradesh, the people are forced to endure hell–nowhere else are there such severe power cuts.” She added, “From Meerut to Ballia, people are distressed. Children, the elderly, women and farmers are all suffering in this scorching heat.”
The IMD has advised residents to avoid prolonged direct sunlight exposure, stay hydrated and limit outdoor activities during peak afternoon hours.
